sense.definition: a raised portion of a road surface designed to slow down vehicles in order to increase safety for pedestrians or other drivers

examples.x: The car slowed down as it approached the speed bump.
There’s a speed bump near the school to ensure drivers don’t go too fast.
The neighborhood streets are filled with speed bumps to reduce traffic speed.
Speed bumps are often placed near parks or playgrounds for safety reasons.
The council plans to install more speed bumps in the residential area.

sense.definition: a raised part of the road designed to make vehicles slow down

examples.x: There is a speed hump near the school to ensure cars drive slowly.
Drivers must be careful when approaching a speed hump to avoid damage to their vehicles.
The new speed hump on our street has made it safer for children to play outside.
Some drivers do not like speed humps because they have to reduce their speed.
The city installed a speed hump to prevent speeding in residential areas.

sense.definition: a sloped pile of dirt next to roads to keep them strong and stop them from wearing away

examples.x: She noticed wildflowers growing on the grassy bank.
The car slid down the steep bank after losing control.
He parked the truck on the roadside bank to change a tire.
They reinforced the bank with concrete to prevent landslides.
The bank was covered in snow after the heavy storm.

sense.definition: a barrier along the edge of a road or bridge to stop cars from going off the road

examples.x: She leaned against the guardrail to enjoy the view.
He noticed damage to the guardrail after the accident.
The guardrail saved the car from plunging into the ravine.
They installed a new guardrail along the sharp curve.
The guardrail was painted bright yellow for visibility.

sense.definition: a type of concrete or plastic barrier used on roads to separate lanes of traffic or to protect construction areas

examples.x: The construction workers placed a K-rail along the highway to keep drivers safe.
The K-rail prevented the car from crossing into the other lane during the accident.

sense.definition: a short post installed on roads and sidewalks to control or direct traffic

examples.x: The bollard marked the boundary of the pedestrian zone.
She locked her bike to the metal bollard.
He maneuvered the forklift around the concrete bollard.
They installed bollards to prevent unauthorized parking.
The bollard had a reflective stripe for nighttime visibility.

sense.definition: a brightly colored cone-shaped object used to guide or warn people about road work or obstacles

examples.x: The workers placed a traffic cone near the hole in the road.
Drivers slowed down when they saw the traffic cone ahead.
A traffic cone blocked the entrance to the parking lot.
Please move the traffic cone so we can park the car here.
The traffic cone was knocked over by the strong wind.

sense.definition: a gate made of horizontal or vertical bars that allows people or animals to see through it while restricting access

examples.x: The farm had a bar gate at the entrance to keep the cows from wandering out.
She leaned against the bar gate to watch the sunset over the fields.
The children slipped through the bar gate to play in the garden.
He installed a new bar gate to increase security around his property.
The park was closed for the night, and a bar gate blocked the main path.

sense.definition: a strong, concrete wall used to separate lanes of traffic or protect areas from vehicles

examples.x: The highway was divided into two sections by a Jersey barrier to keep cars moving in different directions.
During road construction, workers placed a Jersey barrier to keep drivers away from the work zone.
The Jersey barrier helped prevent cars from crossing into oncoming traffic after the accident.
The Jersey barrier was put in place to block the path of vehicles during the parade.

sense.definition: a small, often reflective device on the road used to guide drivers and indicate lanes, especially at night

examples.x: The raised pavement markers on the highway glowed brightly in the car's headlights.
During the foggy night, the raised pavement markers helped the driver stay in his lane.
Workers installed new raised pavement markers on the recently paved road.
The raised pavement markers make it easier to see the lane divisions in the dark.
When the streetlights were out, the raised pavement markers provided some guidance for the drivers.

sense.definition: a series of raised or grooved patterns on the road that make a noise and vibration to alert drivers when they are drifting out of their lane

examples.x: The car shook and made a loud noise as it drove over the rumble strip.
Rumble strips are placed along the edges of highways to prevent accidents.
When the truck hit the rumble strip, the driver quickly corrected his steering.
The loud sound from the rumble strip woke up the sleepy driver.
Installing rumble strips on rural roads can help reduce the number of crashes.

sense.definition: a raised, rounded marker on a road used to guide traffic or mark lanes
a small reflective device on a road that helps drivers see lane markings and edges at night
a gridded metal or concrete structure embedded in the road surface to prevent livestock from crossing

examples.x: She slowed down as she approached the cattle guard.
They installed a cattle guard at the entrance to the ranch.
He heard the cattle guard rattling as the truck passed over it.
The cattle guard was effective in keeping animals off the highway.
They inspected the cattle guard for damage after the winter.

sense.definition: a tall post with a light on top, usually found along roads, streets, or sidewalks

examples.x: Residents petitioned the city council to install additional street lights in their neighborhood to improve safety and security after dark.
The city's sustainability initiative aims to reduce energy consumption by replacing traditional street lights with energy-efficient LED alternatives.

sense.definition: a device connected to a water supply that firefighters use to put out fires

examples.x: The firetruck stopped next to the fire hydrant during the emergency.
The fire hydrant was painted bright red to make it easy to spot.
Firefighters attached a hose to the fire hydrant to extinguish the flames.
Parking near a fire hydrant is illegal in many cities.
The fire hydrant sprayed water when it was accidentally hit by a car.

sense.definition: a raised pile of earth, stone, or concrete along the side of a road to support it or act as a barrier

examples.x: The embankment provided stability to the road.
They reinforced the embankment with retaining walls.
She walked along the grassy embankment beside the highway.
He planted trees on the embankment to prevent erosion.
The embankment was covered in wildflowers in the spring.

sense.definition: a shallow channel at the edge of a road that collects and carries away rainwater

examples.x: The car splashed water from the gutter as it drove by quickly.
Leaves and trash often collect in the gutter, causing it to clog.
Workers clean the gutters regularly to prevent flooding during heavy rains.
A broken gutter can cause water to pool on the road, making it slippery.
After the storm, the gutters were full of mud and debris.

sense.definition: a mandatory annual inspection of vehicle safety, emissions, and roadworthiness in the UK

examples.x: The MOT test certificate was valid for one year.

sense.definition: a scientific test in which a vehicle is crashed to see how safe it is for passengers

examples.x: Engineers use a crash test to check if the car is safe in an accident.
The results of the crash test showed that the new model is very safe.
Before a car can be sold, it must pass a crash test.
Manufacturers improved the car's design after a crash test revealed weaknesses.

sense.definition: a life-sized model of a human used in tests to see how safe cars are in accidents

examples.x: The crash test dummy was placed in the car to check how well the seat belts work.
Engineers study the crash test dummy after an accident to understand what injuries might happen.
The crash test dummy helps car makers improve safety features for drivers and passengers.
During the test, the crash test dummy wore sensors to measure the impact on different parts of the body.
Using a crash test dummy, scientists can find ways to make cars safer in real-life crashes.

sense.definition: a practical examination of a vehicle's performance and handling on actual roads

examples.x: He took the new car for a road test before purchasing it.
The road test included various driving conditions.
The road test route covered city streets and highways.
The road test was executed successfully.
They conducted a road test to evaluate the prototype vehicle.

sense.definition: a suggested maximum speed for vehicles on a specific section of road, usually indicated by a sign, to enhance safety under certain conditions

examples.x: The advisory speed limit on the sharp curve is 30 kilometers per hour to prevent accidents.
Drivers are encouraged to follow the advisory speed limit when the road is wet and slippery.
The mountain pass has an advisory speed limit of 40 kilometers per hour due to the narrow and winding roads.
During heavy fog, the advisory speed limit helps drivers to navigate safely and avoid collisions.
The advisory speed limit on the bridge is lower because of the strong crosswinds that can make driving difficult.

sense.definition: a place on a road where trucks are checked to see how much they weigh

examples.x: Trucks must stop at the weigh station to make sure they are not carrying too much weight.
The driver saw a sign indicating the weigh station was only two miles ahead.
At the weigh station, the officers checked the weight of the truck and its cargo.
If a truck is too heavy, it can be fined at the weigh station.
The weigh station is important for keeping the roads safe and preventing damage.

sense.definition: a special area at the end of a road or runway that helps to stop vehicles or planes that cannot stop on their own

examples.x: The truck driver was relieved when his brakes failed, but he saw the arrester bed ahead.
After the plane skidded on the wet runway, it safely stopped in the arrester bed.
The new highway design includes an arrester bed for added safety.
Emergency crews quickly cleared the arrester bed after the accident.
Drivers should know the location of the nearest arrester bed in case of brake failure.

sense.definition: a structure built over or under roads to help animals safely cross from one side to the other

examples.x: Many wildlife crossings are constructed to reduce accidents involving animals and vehicles on highways.
The new highway project includes several wildlife crossings to protect local wildlife populations.
Wildlife crossings are designed with natural elements to encourage animals to use them instead of crossing dangerous roads.
Drivers are urged to be cautious near wildlife crossings, especially during dawn and dusk when animals are most active.
Research shows that well-placed wildlife crossings can significantly decrease collisions between vehicles and wildlife.

sense.definition: a group of students or adults who help children cross the road safely near the school

examples.x: The school road patrol wears bright vests so drivers can see them easily.
Every morning, the school road patrol stands at the crosswalk to guide the younger students.
Parents feel better knowing that the school road patrol is there to keep their children safe.
The school road patrol uses stop signs to signal when it's safe for children to cross.
During bad weather, the school road patrol is especially important for helping kids cross the slippery roads safely.

sense.definition: a special traffic signal that helps people cross busy streets safely

examples.x: The city installed a pedestrian hybrid beacon near the school to ensure children's safety.
When the pedestrian hybrid beacon is activated, drivers must stop to let pedestrians cross.
A pedestrian hybrid beacon flashes yellow before turning red to alert drivers to slow down.
Pedestrians should press the button to activate the pedestrian hybrid beacon when they want to cross the road.
The pedestrian hybrid beacon helps to reduce accidents at intersections without regular traffic signals.

sense.definition: the awareness, understanding, and ability to navigate safely on roads, often involving knowledge of traffic rules and dangers

examples.x: Good road sense is essential for safe driving.
She demonstrated excellent road sense in heavy traffic.
He taught his teenage son about road sense and defensive driving.
Road sense includes anticipating the actions of other drivers.
They developed a campaign to promote road sense among young drivers.

sense.definition: a hard hat worn by soldiers, bikers, etc. for protection

examples.x: The cyclist adjusted his aerodynamic helmet and set off on the race.
The firefighter’s heavy-duty helmet shielded him from falling debris.
She fastened the chin strap of her bike helmet before pedaling away.

sense.definition: the condition of a vehicle that makes it safe and suitable to be driven on the road

examples.x: Before taking the car on a long trip, ensure its roadworthiness by checking the tires and brakes.
If a vehicle’s roadworthiness is in doubt, it may be banned from being driven on public roads.

sense.definition: an area that a person cannot see or notice

examples.x: The driver checked his blind spot before changing lanes.
She didn't see the cat because it was in her blind spot.
The cyclist was in the truck's blind spot and almost got hit.
There is a blind spot on the side of the mirror where you can't see other cars.

sense.definition: something that blocks or impedes movement on a road

examples.x: The fallen tree was an obstruction on the road.
She reported the obstruction to the highway patrol.
He maneuvered around the road obstruction carefully.
They removed the obstruction to clear the lane for traffic.
The road obstruction caused a traffic backup.

sense.definition: a barrier or obstruction placed across a road, often by authorities, to control or stop traffic

examples.x: The police set up a roadblock to catch the fleeing suspect.
She encountered a roadblock on the highway due to construction.
He waited patiently in line at the roadblock.
They detoured around the roadblock to reach their destination.
The roadblock was lifted after the accident was cleared.

sense.definition: to restrict access to a particular area by using a barrier

examples.x: After the accident, the police quickly arrived to cordon off the accident site for investigation.
During the VIP event, security personnel worked to cordon off the restricted areas from the general public.
The fire department had to cordon off the building due to a gas leak, ensuring the safety of surrounding areas.
